name: serialize_preserves_prefix
reason: The serialize ensures that the output grows by exactly the serialization length — but this is trivially true for u64 (always 8 bytes) and the trait-level ensures is `external_body` for all other types, so the append-only guarantee is unverified for usize, Vec<u8>, and Vec<T>

name: usize_serialize_external_body_unchecked
reason: `usize::serialize` is `external_body` with `unimplemented!()` — the exec implementation is completely missing, yet the spec ensures (prefix preservation + appended serialization) are trusted without any verification

name: vec_u8_serialize_length_prefix_then_data
code:
proof fn phi_3_vec_u8_serialize_length_prefix_then_data(v: Vec<u8>)
    requires
        v.is_marshalable(),
    ensures
        v.ghost_serialize().len() == (v@.len() as usize).ghost_serialize().len() + v@.len(),
{
}
reason: `Vec<u8>` serialization is length-prefixed then raw bytes — but the `serialize` exec fn is `external_body` with `unimplemented!()`, so there's no guarantee the runtime behavior matches this spec

name: trait_serialize_external_body_default
reason: u64 serialization is injective (distinct values produce distinct byte sequences) — but this injectivity is only derivable from `lemma_auto_spec_u64_to_from_le_bytes` and is never stated as a trait-level guarantee, so other Marshalable impls have no obligation to be injective

name: vec_t_serialize_external_body_no_verification
code:
proof fn phi_5_vec_t_serialize_external_body_no_verification(v: Vec<u64>)
    requires
        v.is_marshalable(),
    ensures
        v.ghost_serialize().len() >= (v@.len() as usize).ghost_serialize().len(),
{
}
reason: `Vec<T>::serialize` is `external_body` — the spec says serialization is length-prefix + fold of element serializations, but the exec implementation is `unimplemented!()`, so the ensures clauses (prefix preservation, correct appended bytes) are entirely unverified at runtime
